incorrects.description: Student selected 2 × 3: reversed the order of groups and items per group

params.description: First bag highlights, showing the 2 items inside it.

steps.text: You're working on it. The first number tells us how many bags. Count the bags, not what's inside them.

incorrects.description: Student selected 3 + 2: used addition instead of multiplication

params.description: All bags highlight briefly, then the items inside highlight.

steps.text: Let's think about this together. We're not just adding 3 and 2. We have 3 bags with 2 in each bag. That's what × means.

incorrects.description: Student selected 2 + 3: used addition and reversed the order

params.description: Bags pulse one at a time, showing equal groups structure.

steps.text: Here's a hint: when every group has the same number, we use ×. Count the bags for the first number.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System highlights all 3 bags, then highlights the 2 items in one bag.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. I count the bags: 1, 2, 3. That's my first number. Then I check one bag: 2 items. That's my second number. The answer is 3 × 2. Three bags, two in each.
